title: Data Structures
description: Fundamental data structures including arrays, linked lists, trees, graphs, and hash tables with complexity analysis.

modules.title: Getting Started
modules.description: Introduction and overview of Data Structures

modules.title: Core Lectures
modules.description: All lectures and concept pages for this course

pages.title: Complexity Analysis: Big-O, Theta, Omega, and Space Complexity
Recursion: Base Cases, Call Stack, and Tail Recursion Optimization
Dynamic Arrays: Amortized Analysis and ArrayList Internals
String Operations: Manipulation, Pattern Matching, and StringBuilder
Matrix Operations: 2D Arrays, Rotation, Transposition, and Spiral Traversal
Singly Linked List: Insertion, Deletion, Reversal, and Floyd’s Cycle Detection
Doubly and Circular Linked Lists: Operations and LRU Cache Implementation
Stack Implementation: Array and Linked List Backed, Monotonic Stack Uses
Queue and Deque: Circular Buffer, Priority Queue, and Sliding Window Maximum
Binary Tree Fundamentals: Properties, Traversals, and Reconstruction
Binary Search Tree Operations: Search, Insert, Delete, and Successor
AVL Trees: Rotation Operations, Balance Factor, and Height Analysis
Red-Black Trees: Color Properties, Insertions, and Deletions
B-Trees and B+ Trees: Disk-Based Storage and Database Indexing
Trie (Prefix Tree): Insert, Search, Autocomplete, and Compressed Trie
Segment Trees: Range Sum, Range Minimum, and Lazy Propagation
Fenwick Tree (Binary Indexed Tree): Point Updates and Prefix Queries
Heap Operations: Heapify, Extract-Min/Max, Heap Sort, and k-th Largest
Hash Table Internals: Hash Functions, Chaining, and Open Addressing
Bloom Filters and Probabilistic Data Structures: Count-Min Sketch
Graph Representation: Adjacency Matrix, List, and Edge List Comparison
BFS and DFS: Traversal, Connected Components, and Cycle Detection
Union-Find (Disjoint Sets): Union by Rank and Path Compression
Skip List: Probabilistic Layered Structure and O(log n) Operations
Van Emde Boas Trees and Integer Data Structure Optimizations
Persistent Data Structures: Immutable Updates and Functional Trees
External Memory Data Structures: Buffer Trees and Cache-Oblivious Layouts
